issues.title: QA contract gate left an unresolved repair plan (status=manual_review, 2 item(s)): infobox

fix_recommendation.summary: The Stage-3 contract gate flagged section drift that was not cleared. Re-render from fragments, or — if the flagged section is depth-conditional (§3 walkthroughs / §7 security-architecture at --quick) — this is a checker bug.
fix_recommendation.rationale: A lingering .qa-repair-plan.json means check_contract found an expected section missing (or out of order). Genuine drift is fixed by recomposing from the fragments. But the contract gate evaluates depth/skip conditions: §3 and §7 are intentionally suppressed at --quick, so a plan naming ONLY those is a false positive. Flagged items: infobox.

actions.details: Inspect the `actions[].heading` list — are the named sections genuinely expected at this depth?
Recompose from the existing fragments when the drift is real (a fragment was edited or a renderer/contract change landed).

issues.title: QA status did not reach pass (status=repair_required)

fix_recommendation.summary: QA status is 'repair_required' (not pass) — the document shipped with an unresolved QA concern.
fix_recommendation.rationale: The deterministic gate or the QA reviewer wrote a non-pass status. The report still exists but a check (contract, mermaid, placeholders, yaml↔md consistency) did not clear.

actions.details: Read the status note + any referenced repair plan to see which check failed.
